title: Digastrium fragile
taxonomy: Digastrium fragile is classified as a genus within the family Pedaliaceae. This family belongs to the order Lamiales, which consists of flowering plants that include snapdragons, mint, and foxgloves. The Pedaliaceae family includes plants native to Africa, Australia, and Asia, and is characterized by their unique flowers and seed pods. Digastrium fragile is one of the species within this family, but it is considered rare and has limited information available. The classification of Digastrium fragile is still being studied and researched by botanists and taxonomists.
